pokemon:pancham.name: Pancham
pokemon:pancham.summary: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head.
There’s no point to the leaf in its mouth, aside from an effort to look cool. It’s mischievous, so it’s not well suited to inexperienced Trainers.
It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.
Wanting to make sure it’s taken seriously, Pancham’s always giving others a glare. But if it’s not focusing, it ends up smiling.
It grows up imitating the behavior of Pangoro, which it looks up to as a leader.
It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master’s actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.
It’s desperate to intimidate its opponents. Be nice and pretend to be scared if you catch it glaring at you intensely.
It follows Pangoro around like a henchman. When Pancham makes a big mistake, its leaf gets taken away.
It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.
It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can’t help grinning if anyone pats its head.

pokemon:pangoro.name: Pangoro
pokemon:pangoro.summary: It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole.
From the slight twitches of its bamboo leaf, it deduces its opponent’s movements. It’s eager to tussle but kindhearted toward its companions.
Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won’t put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.
Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.
It boasts superb physical strength. Those who wish to become Pangoro’s Trainer have no choice but to converse with their fists.
This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.
Pangoro is the type to talk with its fists. It doesn’t say much as it pummels opponents with enough force to send a dump truck flying.
This rowdy Pokémon boasts great physical strength. Many Trainers are also smitten by its lively character.
Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won’t put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.
It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole.
